title: centaurea × maramuresensis
intro: Centaurea × maramuresensis, also known as Maramureș cornflower, is a hybrid plant species of the genus Centaurea. It is a perennial plant native to the Maramureș region in Romania, known for its bright blue flowers and potential medicinal properties. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Centaurea × maramuresensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine in Eastern Europe.
